summary: Reporter gets a NullReferenceException when calling SKImage.Encode() with formats other than JPEG, PNG, or WebP (e.g., HEIF, AVIF, BMP, GIF), not realizing that Skia only supports encoding to JPEG, PNG, and WebP.

stepsToReproduce: Create an SKBitmap and draw on it with SKCanvas
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: ASP.NET Core, .NET 8, SkiaSharp 2.88.8, Windows 10, Visual Studio 2022

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: SKPixmap.Encode switch statement only handles JPEG, PNG, and WebP; this has not changed between 2.88.x and current versions. The issue is not a regression — the same version is listed as both current and last known good.

analysis.summary: Reporter expects SKImage.Encode() to support all SKEncodedImageFormat values, but Skia only provides encoders for JPEG, PNG, and WebP. Passing other formats causes Encode() to return null, then calling SaveTo() on null throws NullReferenceException.
analysis.rationale: The confusion arises because SKEncodedImageFormat is used for both decoding (identifying the format of input data) and encoding (specifying output format), but only JPEG, PNG, and WebP are supported for encoding. The maintainer (mattleibow) confirmed in issue #64 that this is expected behavior from upstream Skia. A community member confirmed the same for this issue. The same version is listed as both current and last known good, confirming this is not a regression.

codeInvestigation.finding: SKPixmap.Encode(SKWStream, SKEncodedImageFormat, int) uses a switch that handles only Jpeg, Png, and Webp — all other formats fall through to '_ => false', causing Encode(format, quality) to return null

SKEncodedImageFormat enum has 14 values (Bmp=0, Gif=1, Ico=2, Jpeg=3, Png=4, Wbmp=5, Webp=6, Pkm=7, Ktx=8, Astc=9, Dng=10, Heif=11, Avif=12, Jpegxl=13) — these represent formats Skia can decode/read, not all formats it can encode
SKImage.Encode(SKEncodedImageFormat, int) delegates to pixmap?.Encode(format, quality) — returns null if pixmap is null or if SKPixmap.Encode returns null for unsupported formats

keySignals.text: This is not a bug. The encodings for those formats are not implemented by Skia.
keySignals.interpretation: Community member correctly identifies this as by-design behavior from upstream Skia

keySignals.text: This issue does not occur when using formats such as JPG, PNG, and WEBP.
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter has correctly identified that only JPEG, PNG, and WebP work for encoding

keySignals.text: Last Known Good Version of SkiaSharp: 2.88.8 (Deprecated)
keySignals.interpretation: Same as current version — this was never working, not a regression

workarounds: Use only JPEG, PNG, or WebP for SKImage.Encode() — these are the only formats Skia supports for encoding
Always null-check the return value of Encode() before calling SaveTo()
For HEIF/AVIF output, encode to JPEG/PNG first, then use a separate platform library for conversion if needed

resolution.hypothesis: Reporter is passing SKEncodedImageFormat.Heif (and other unsupported formats) to SKImage.Encode(), which returns null for any format other than JPEG, PNG, or WebP, causing NullReferenceException.

proposals.title: Use a supported encoding format with proper disposal
proposals.description: Switch to JPEG, PNG, or WebP and wrap the returned SKData in a using statement.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Use a supported encoding format with proper disposal
resolution.recommendedReason: Skia only encodes to JPEG, PNG, and WebP — this is an upstream constraint. The workaround is immediate and correct; using var ensures SKData is properly disposed.

actionability.reason: Behavior is by design from upstream Skia — only JPEG, PNG, and WebP are supported for encoding. Confirmed by maintainer in issue #64 and by a community member in this issue. The same version is listed as both current and last known good, confirming no regression.

actions.description: Correct label from type/bug to type/question, add area/SkiaSharp and os/Windows-Classic
Explain that only JPEG, PNG, and WebP are supported for encoding and provide workaround
Link to the original related issue #64 which covers the same by-design behavior
Close as not a bug — confirmed by-design Skia limitation
